title: Power and/or alarming security system for electrical appliances
abstract: The present disclosure relates generally to security systems and, more particularly, to a power and/or alarming security system for electrical appliances. In an example embodiment, an apparatus comprises a socket and a circuit. The socket can be configured for detachable electrical connection with an electrical appliance. The circuit can be configured to (1) provide power to a connected electrical appliance through the socket, and (2) calibrate an alarm limit for a security function for the socket based on a plurality of electrical characteristics of the connected electrical appliance that are measured via the socket, wherein the electrical characteristics comprise an admittance characteristic and a current draw characteristic of the connected electrical appliance. Furthermore, the circuit can calibrate the alarm limit for the socket according to a plurality of calibration phases.
claims:
Claims WHAT IS CLAIMED IS: 1. An apparatus comprising: a socket configured for detachable electrical connection with an electrical appliance; and a circuit configured to (1) provide power to a connected electrical appliance through the socket, and (2) calibrate an alarm limit for a security function for the socket based on a plurality of electrical characteristics of the connected electrical appliance that are measured via the socket, wherein the electrical characteristics comprise an admittance characteristic and a current draw characteristic of the connected electrical appliance. 2. The apparatus of claim 1 wherein the circuit is further configured to monitor at least one of the admittance characteristic and/or the current draw characteristic of the connected electrical appliance as against the alarm limit to test for an alarm condition. 3. The apparatus of claim 2 wherein the circuit is further configured to (1) measure an admittance characteristic of the connected electrical appliance and a current draw characteristic of the connected electrical appliance through the socket, (2) calibrate an alarm limit with respect to the connected electrical appliance based on the measured admittance characteristic and the measured current draw characteristic, (3) monitor the electrical characteristics of the connected electrical appliance through the socket, (4) compare the monitored electrical characteristics with the calibrated alarm limit to determine whether a security condition exists, and (5) generate a signal indicative of the alarm condition in response to a determination that the security condition exists. 4. The apparatus of claim 3 wherein the circuit is further configured to (1) apply a plurality of different stimulation signals to the connected electrical appliance through the socket, and (2) measure the admittance and current draw characteristics of the connected electrical appliance in response to the different stimulation signals, and (3) define the alarm limit based on the measured admittance and current draw characteristic responses. 5. The apparatus of claim 4 wherein the stimulation signals include a plurality of stimulation signals of different frequencies. 6. The apparatus of any of claims 3-5 wherein the circuit comprises a processor configured to perform the compare and generate operations. 7. The apparatus of any of claims 2-6 wherein the circuit comprises a current and admittance sensor circuit configured to sense an admittance and a current draw of the connected electrical appliance to support the calibrate and monitor operations. 8. The apparatus of claim 7 wherein the circuit further comprises an analog to digital converter (ADC), the ADC configured to digitize the sensed admittance characteristic and the sensed current draw characteristic. 9. The apparatus of claim 8 wherein the circuit further comprises a digital signal processor (DSP) configured to process digitized admittance and the digitized current draw and compute a plurality of admittance characteristic measurements and a plurality of current draw characteristic measurements. 10. The apparatus of claim 9 wherein the circuit further comprises a processor configured to (1) receive the computed admittance characteristic measurements and the computed current draw characteristic measurements from the DSP, (2) calibrate the alarm limit based on a plurality of the computed admittance characteristic measurements and a plurality of the computed current draw characteristic measurements, and (3) compare another plurality of the computed admittance characteristic measurements and another plurality of the computed current draw characteristic measurements with the customized alarm limit to determine whether the alarm condition exists. 11. The apparatus of any of claims 1-10 wherein the circuit is further configured to (1) categorize between a plurality of different types of electrical appliances as part of the calibration, and (2) customize the alarm limit based on the categorized electrical appliance type. 12. The apparatus of claim 11 wherein the electrical appliance types include a cabled electrical appliance having an active standby mode when the electrical appliance is turned off. 13. The apparatus of any of claims 11-12 wherein the electrical appliance types include a first power brick type. 14. The apparatus of any of claims 11-13 wherein the electrical appliance types include a second power brick type. 15. The apparatus of claim 14 wherein the second power brick type comprises a USB charger. 16. The apparatus of any of claims 11-15 wherein the electrical appliance types include a cabled electrical appliance that does not have an active standby mode when the electrical appliance is turned off 17. The apparatus of any of claims 1-16 wherein the socket comprises a plurality of the sockets, each socket connected to the circuit. 18. The apparatus of claim 17 wherein the circuit is further configured to perform the calibrate operations on a per-socket basis such that alarm limits for the sockets are independently customized based on the electrical appliances connected to the sockets. 19. The apparatus of any of claims 1-18 wherein the circuit is configured to calibrate alarm limit for the socket according to a plurality of calibration phases. 20. The apparatus of claim 19 wherein the calibration phases comprise a first calibration phase and a second calibration phase; wherein for the first calibration phase, (1) if a power brick is connected to the socket, the electrical appliance is not connected to the power brick, and (2) if the 

 electrical appliance is not connected to the socket via a power brick, the electrical appliance is connected to the socket and is turned off to be in an unpowered state; and wherein for the second calibration phase, (1) if a power brick is connected to the socket, the electrical appliance is connected to the power brick, and (2) if the electrical appliance is not connected to the socket via a power brick, the electrical appliance is connected to the socket and is turned off to be in an unpowered state. 21. The apparatus of any of claims 19-20 wherein the calibrated alarm limit is insensitive to whether the connected electrical appliance is powered. 22. The apparatus of any of claims 19-21 wherein the calibrated alarm limit is sensitive to whether electrical appliance is plugged into the socket via a power brick. 23. The apparatus of any of claims 1-22 wherein the circuit comprises a digital signal processor (DSP) configured to process data representative of the monitored electrical characteristics. 24. The apparatus of claim 23 wherein the DSP comprises a field programmable gate array (FPGA). 25. The apparatus of any of claims 1-24 wherein the circuit comprises a current and admittance sensor circuit configured to sense the admittance and the current draw characteristics of the connected electrical appliance. 26. The apparatus of any of claims 1-25 wherein the socket is configured to receive power from an external AC power source. 27. The apparatus of claim 26 further comprising a surge protector circuit that provides surge protection for the socket. 28. The apparatus of any of claims 1-27 wherein the circuit is configured to be switchable between an armed state and a disarmed state. 29. The apparatus of claim 28 further comprising an interface, the interface configured to receive authorization data from a user for controlling whether the circuit is in the armed state or disarmed state. 30. The apparatus of claim 29 wherein the interface comprises an RFID sensor. 31. The apparatus of any of claims 1-30 further comprising a status indicator, wherein the status indicator indicates any of a plurality of different operating states for the power strip, the operating states including a calibration state, an armed state, a disarmed state, and an alarm condition state. 32. The apparatus of claim 31 wherein the status indicator comprises a plurality of the status indicators. 33. The apparatus of any of claims 1-32 further comprising a wireless transceiver for connecting the apparatus with a wireless network, and wherein the wireless transceiver is configured to wirelessly transmit the generated signal to a remote computer system via the wireless network. 34. The apparatus of claim 33 wherein the circuit is further configured to process and store status data about the connected electrical appliance, and wherein the wireless transceiver is configured to wirelessly transmit the status data to the remote computer system via the wireless network. 35. The apparatus of any of claims 33-34 wherein the wireless transceiver is further configured to wirelessly receive a command from the remote computer system via the wireless network, and wherein the circuit is further configured to control the circuit based on the received command. 36. The apparatus of claim 35 wherein the command comprises a command to disarm or arm the socket. 37. The apparatus of any of claims 1-36 further comprising an interface configured to interface the circuit with an expansion module, and wherein the circuit is further configured to communicate data about the socket to the expansion module via the interface. 38. The apparatus of claim 37 further comprising the expansion module, wherein the expansion module comprises (1) a first expansion module interface configured to interface with the interface, and (2) a second expansion module interface configured to interface with an external speaker for sounding an alarm in response to the generated signal. 39. The apparatus of claim 38 wherein the expansion module further comprises (1) a third expansion module interface configured to interface with an external sensor for receiving authorization data from a user, and (2) a circuit configured to pass authorization data received via the third expansion module interface to the circuit via the first expansion module interface. 40. The apparatus of any of claims 37-39 wherein the expansion module is further configured to connect the circuit with a plurality of remote sockets. 41. The apparatus of any of claims 37-40 wherein the expansion module comprises a status indicator, wherein the status indicator indicates any of a plurality of different operating states for the apparatus, the operating states including a calibration state, an armed state, a disarmed state, and an alarm condition state. 42. The apparatus of any of claims 37-41 wherein the expansion module comprises a speaker for sounding an alarm in response to the generated signal. 43. The apparatus of any of claims 37-42 wherein the expansion module comprises (1) a sensor for receiving authorization data from a user, and (2) a circuit configured to 

 pass authorization data received via the third expansion module interface to the circuit via the first expansion module interface. 44. The apparatus of any of claims 1-43 wherein the circuit is further configured to (1) receive AC power from a power source, and (2) provide AC power to the connected electrical appliance through the socket. 45. The apparatus of any of claims 1-44 wherein the socket and the circuit are part of a power strip. 46. The apparatus of claim 45 wherein the power strip comprises a plurality of the sockets connected to the circuit. 47. The apparatus of any of claims 1-44 wherein the socket and the circuit are part of a wall outlet. 48. The apparatus of any of claims 1-47 wherein the socket comprises a USB-A socket. 49. The apparatus of any of claims 1-47 wherein the socket comprises a USB-C socket. 50. The apparatus of any of claims 1-47 wherein the socket comprises an RJ-45 socket. 51. The apparatus of any of claims 1-47 wherein the socket comprises a power over Ethernet (PoE) socket. 52. The apparatus of any of claims 1-51 wherein the circuit is configured to differentially measure the admittance characteristic through the socket to provide noise reduction with respect to the measured admittance characteristic. 53. The apparatus of any of claims 1-52 wherein the circuit is further configured to generate a pseudo noise (PN) sequence for spreading a spectrum of stimulation signals that are applied by the circuit to the socket to support the measured admittance characteristic and the measured current draw characteristic. 54. A method comprising: detecting a connection of an electrical appliance to a socket through a power brick, wherein the power brick is detachably connected to the socket, and wherein the electrical appliance is detachably connected to the power brick; calibrating an alarm limit for the socket based on the detected connection, wherein the calibrated alarm limit is sensitive to both (1) a disconnection of the electrical appliance from the power brick while the power brick remains connected to the socket, and (2) a disconnection of the power brick and the electrical appliance from the socket; measuring a current draw characteristic of the electrical appliance connected to the socket via the power brick; comparing the measured current draw characteristic with the alarm limit; and generating an alarm signal in response to the comparison indicating that the electrical appliance is no longer connected to the socket. 55. The method of claim 54 wherein the power brick comprises a power brick that draws current when connected to the socket even if no electrical appliance is connected to the power brick. 56. The method of claim 55 wherein the detecting step comprises: measuring a calibration current draw characteristic of the power brick connected to the socket when the electrical appliance is not connected to the power brick; and measuring a calibration current draw characteristic of the power brick connected to the socket when the electrical appliance is connected to the power brick; and wherein the calibrating step comprises defining the alarm limit to be a value that is (1) above the measured calibration current draw characteristic of the power brick connected to the socket when the electrical appliance is not connected to the power brick, 

 and (2) below the measured calibration current draw characteristic of the power brick connected to the socket when the electrical appliance is connected to the power brick. 57. The method of claim 56 wherein the defined alarm limit comprises a value that is 110% of the measured calibration current draw characteristic of the power brick connected to the socket when the electrical appliance is not connected to the power brick. 58. The method of any of claims 56-57 wherein the detecting step further comprises; measuring a calibration admittance characteristic of the power brick connected to the socket when the electrical appliance is not connected to the power brick; and categorizing the socket as being connected to the electrical appliance via the power brick based on a plurality of tests applied to (1) the measured calibration admittance characteristic of the power brick connected to the socket when the electrical appliance is not connected to the power brick, (2) the measured calibration current draw characteristic of the power brick connected to the socket when the electrical appliance is not connected to the power brick, and (3) the measured calibration current draw characteristic of the power brick connected to the socket when the electrical appliance is connected to the power brick. 59. The method of claim 58 wherein the calibrating step further comprises: calibrating a second alarm limit for the socket based on the measured calibration admittance characteristic of the power brick connected to the socket when the electrical appliance is not connected to the power brick; measuring an admittance characteristic of the electrical appliance connected to the socket via the power brick; comparing the measured admittance characteristic with the second alarm limit; and wherein the generating step comprises generating the alarm signal in response to either (1) the current draw characteristic comparison indicating that the electrical appliance has been disconnected from the power brick or that the power brick has been disconnected from the socket, or (2) the admittance characteristic comparison indicating that the measured admittance characteristic is below the second alarm limit. 60. The method of claim 54 wherein the power brick comprises a power brick that draws negligible current when connected to the socket and no electrical appliance is connected to the power brick. 61. The method of claim 60 wherein the calibrating step comprises defining the alarm limit to be a value that corresponds to a current draw floor for the power brick. 62. The method of claim 61 wherein the detecting step further comprises; measuring a calibration admittance characteristic of the power brick connected to the socket when the electrical appliance is not connected to the power brick; and measuring a calibration current draw characteristic of the power brick connected to the socket when the electrical appliance is connected to the power brick; measuring a calibration current draw characteristic of the power brick connected to the socket when the electrical appliance is not connected to the power brick; categorizing the socket as being connected to the electrical appliance via the power brick based on a plurality of tests applied to (1) the measured calibration admittance characteristic of the power brick connected to the socket when the electrical appliance is not connected to the power brick, (2) the measured calibration current draw characteristic of the power brick connected to the socket when the electrical appliance is not connected to the power brick, and (3) the measured calibration current draw characteristic of the power brick connected to the socket when the electrical appliance is connected to the power brick. 63. The method of claim 62 wherein the calibrating step further comprises: calibrating a second alarm limit for the socket based on the measured calibration admittance characteristic of the power brick connected to the socket when the electrical appliance is not connected to the power brick; measuring an admittance characteristic of the electrical appliance connected to the socket via the power brick; comparing the measured admittance characteristic with the second alarm limit; and 

 wherein the generating step comprises generating the alarm signal in response to either (1) the current draw characteristic comparison indicating that the electrical appliance has been disconnected from the power brick or that the power brick has been disconnected from the socket, or (2) the admittance characteristic comparison indicating that the measured admittance characteristic is below the second alarm limit. 64. The method of any of claims 60-63 wherein the power brick comprises a USB charger. 65. The method of any of claims 54-64 further comprising any combination of features set forth in any of claims 1-53. 66. An apparatus configured to perform the method of any of claims 54-65. 67. A method comprising: detecting a connection of an electrical appliance to a socket through a cable; calibrating an alarm limit for the socket based on the detected connection, wherein the calibrated alarm limit is sensitive to both (1) a disconnection of the electrical appliance from the cable or a cutting of the cable while the cable remains connected to the socket, and (2) a disconnection of the cable and the electrical appliance from the socket; measuring an admittance characteristic of the electrical appliance connected to the socket via the cable; comparing the measured admittance characteristic with the alarm limit; and generating an alarm signal in response to the comparison indicating that the electrical appliance is no longer connected to the socket. 68. The method of claim 67 wherein the electrical appliance has an active standby mode that draws current even when the connected electrical appliance is turned off. 69. The method of claim 68 wherein the detecting step comprises: measuring a calibration admittance characteristic of the connected electrical appliance; and 

 wherein the calibrating step comprises defining the alarm limit based on the measured calibration admittance characteristic. 70. The method of claim 69 wherein the detecting step further comprises: measuring a calibration current draw characteristic of the connected electrical appliance; and wherein the calibrating step comprises calibrating a second alarm limit for the socket based on the measured calibration current draw characteristic of the connected electrical appliance. 71. The method of claim 70 wherein the second alarm limit calibrating step comprises defining the second alarm limit to be a value that is below the measured calibration current draw characteristic of the connected electrical appliance, the method further comprising: measuring a current draw characteristic of the connected electrical appliance; comparing the measured current draw characteristic with the second alarm limit; and wherein the generating step comprises generating the alarm signal in response to either (1) the current draw characteristic comparison indicating that the electrical appliance is no longer connected to the socket, or (2) the admittance characteristic comparison indicating that electrical appliance is no longer connected to the socket. 72. The method of claim 71 wherein the calibration current draw characteristic measuring step comprises: measuring a current draw characteristic of the connected electrical appliance for a first calibration phase; measuring a current draw characteristic of the connected electrical appliance for a second calibration phase; and categorizing the socket as being connected to the electrical appliance having the active standby mode that draws current even when the connected electrical appliance is turned off based on a plurality of tests applied to (1) the measured calibration admittance characteristic of the connected electrical appliance, (2) the measured first calibration 

 phase current draw characteristic of the connected electrical appliance, and (3) the measured second calibration phase current draw characteristic of the connected electrical appliance. 73. The method of claim 72 wherein the defined second alarm limit comprises a value that is 90% of the measured first calibration phase current draw characteristic of the connected electrical appliance. 74. The method of claim 67 wherein the electrical appliance does not have an active standby mode that draws current even when the connected electrical appliance is turned off. 75. The method of claim 74 detecting step comprises: measuring a calibration admittance characteristic of the connected electrical appliance; measuring a current draw characteristic of the connected electrical appliance for a first calibration phase; measuring a current draw characteristic of the connected electrical appliance for a second calibration phase; and categorizing the socket as being connected to the electrical appliance that does not have the active standby mode that draws current even when the connected electrical appliance is turned off based on a plurality of tests applied to (1) the measured calibration admittance characteristic of the connected electrical appliance, (2) the measured first calibration phase current draw characteristic of the connected electrical appliance, and (3) the measured second calibration phase current draw characteristic of the connected electrical appliance. 76. A method comprising: detecting a connection of an electrical appliance to a socket through a cable; calibrating an alarm limit for the socket based on the detected connection, wherein the calibrated alarm limit is sensitive to both (1) a disconnection of the electrical 

 appliance from the cable or a cutting of the cable while the cable remains connected to the socket, and (2) a disconnection of the cable and the electrical appliance from the socket; measuring a current draw characteristic of the electrical appliance connected to the socket via the cable; comparing the measured current draw characteristic with the alarm limit; and generating an alarm signal in response to the comparison indicating that the electrical appliance is no longer connected to the socket. 77. The method of any of claims 67-76 further comprising any combination of features set forth in any of claims 1-66. 78. An apparatus configured to perform the method of any of claims 67-77. 79. A method comprising: distinguishing between a socket connected to an electrical appliance via a power brick and a socket connected to an electrical appliance via a cable based on a plurality of admittance characteristic measurements and a plurality of current draw characteristic measurements through the socket over a plurality of calibration phases; and calibrating an alarm limit for the socket based on the distinguishing step. 80. The method of claim 79 further comprising any combination of features set forth in any of claims 1-78. 81. An apparatus configured to perform the method of any of claims 79-80. 82. A method comprising: calibrating a security function for a socket connected to an electrical appliance, wherein the calibrating step comprises: measuring a calibration admittance of the electrical appliance connected to the socket; measuring a calibration current draw of the electrical appliance connected to the socket; 

 defining a threshold based on the measured calibration admittance and the measured calibration current draw; and monitoring the socket in accordance with the calibrated security function, wherein the monitoring step comprises: measuring a test admittance of the electrical appliance connected to the socket; measuring a test current draw of the electrical appliance connected to the socket; comparing at least one of the measured test admittance and/or the measured test current draw with the threshold to test whether a security condition exists; and generating a signal in response to the comparing step resulting in a determination that the security condition exists. 83. The method of claim 82 wherein the generated signal is indicative of a removal of the electrical appliance from the socket. 84. The method of any of claims 82-83 further comprising: repeating the method steps in response to a different electrical appliance being connected with the socket. 85. The method of any of claims 82-84 further comprising: repeating the method steps with respect to a plurality of the sockets, each socket connected to a different electrical appliance and having its own customized defined threshold. 86. The method of any of claims 82-85 wherein the calibrating step further comprises: applying a plurality of different stimulation signals to the connected electrical appliance through the socket; and measuring the calibration admittance and the calibration current draw in response to the applied stimulation signals. 87. The method of claim 86 wherein the stimulation signals comprise a plurality of stimulation signals of different frequencies. 88. The method of any of claims 82-87 wherein the calibrating step comprises performing the calibration admittance measuring and the calibration current draw measuring while the electrical appliance is (1) connected to the socket and (2) in an unpowered state. 89. The method of any of claims 82-88 wherein the electrical appliance is connected to the socket via a power brick, wherein the electrical appliance is removable from the power brick; wherein the calibrating step comprises: measuring a calibration admittance of the power brick connected to the socket while the electrical appliance is not connected to the power brick; measuring a calibration current draw of the power brick connected to the socket while the electrical appliance is not connected to the power brick; measuring a calibration current draw of the power brick and the electrical appliance while the electrical appliance is connected to the power brick; and defining the threshold based on (1) the measured calibration admittance of the power brick connected to the socket while the electrical appliance is not connected to the power brick, (2) the measured calibration current draw of the power brick connected to the socket while the electrical appliance is not connected to the power brick, and (3) the measured calibration current draw of the power brick and the electrical appliance while the electrical appliance is connected to the power brick. 90. The method of any of claims 82-89 further comprising: performing the calibrating step in response to a user request. 91. The method of claim 90 further comprising: authenticating the user request as being from an authorized user; and 

 performing the calibrating step in response to the authentication of the user request. 92. The method of any of claims 82-91 further comprising: arming the security function in response to a user request. 93. The method of claim 92 further comprising: authenticating the user request as being from an authorized user; and performing the arming step in response to the authentication of the user request. 94. The method of any of claims 82-93 further comprising: generating an audible alarm in response to the generated signal. 95. The method of any of claims 82-94 further comprising: generating a visual indicator in response to the generated signal. 96. The method of any of claims 82-95 further comprising: communicating a notification message to a user in response to the generated signal. 97. The method of any of claims 82-96 wherein the socket is part of a power strip. 98. The method of any of claims 82-96 wherein the socket is part of a wall outlet. 99. An apparatus comprising: a socket configured for detachable electrical connection with a power brick, the power brick configured for detachable electrical connection with an electrical appliance; and a circuit configured to (1) provide power through the socket, and (2) calibrate a security threshold of the socket according to a plurality of calibration phases, (3) monitor an electrical characteristic of a connected power brick and an electrical appliance connected to the power brick through the socket, (4) compare the monitored electrical 

 characteristic with the calibrated threshold to determine whether a security condition exists, and (5) generate a signal indicative of the security condition in response to a determination that the security condition exists; wherein the calibration phases comprise (1) a first calibration phase where stimulation signals are applied to the socket and electrical characteristic responses are measured from the socket when the power brick is connected to the socket but an electrical appliance is not connected to the power brick, and (2) a second calibration phase where stimulation signals are applied to the socket and electrical characteristic responses are measured from the socket when the power brick is connected to the socket and the electrical appliance is connected to the power brick. 100. The apparatus of claim 99 further comprising any combination of features set forth in claims 1-98. 101. A method comprising: connecting a power brick to a socket, wherein the power brick is not connected to an electrical appliance; while the power brick is connected to the socket and the electrical appliance is not connected to the power brick, (1) applying a plurality of stimulation signals to the power brick through the socket, and (2) measuring a plurality of electrical characteristics of the power brick in response to the stimulation signals; connecting the electrical appliance to the power brick; while the power brick is connected to the socket and the electrical appliance is connected to the power brick, (1) applying a plurality of stimulation signals to the power brick and electronic device through the socket, and (2) measuring a plurality of electrical characteristics of the power brick and electronic device in response to the stimulation signals; defining a security threshold based on (1) the measured electrical characteristics of the power brick in response to the stimulation signals and (2) the measured electrical characteristics of the power brick and electronic device in response to the stimulation signals; and testing a security status of the socket based on the defined security threshold. 102. The method of claim 101 further comprising: in response to the testing, detecting a disconnection between the electrical appliance and the power brick even if the power brick remains connected to the socket. 103. The method of any of claims 101-102 further comprising: in response to the testing, detecting a disconnection between the power brick and the socket. 104. The method of any of claims 101-103 further comprising any combination of features set forth in claims 1-98. 105. An apparatus comprising: a socket configured for detachable electrical connection with an electrical appliance; and a circuit configured to (1) provide power to a connected electrical appliance through the socket, (2) determine a categorization type of the connected electrical appliance based on measurements of a plurality of electrical characteristics of the connected electrical appliance, and (3) control a security function for the socket based on the determined categorization type. 106. The apparatus of claim 105 further comprising any combination of features set forth in claims 1-104. 107. A method comprising: providing power to a connected electrical appliance through a socket; measuring a plurality of electrical characteristics of the connected electrical appliance through the socket; determining a categorization type of the connected electrical appliance based on the measurements; and controlling a security function for the socket based on the determined categorization type. 108. The method of claim 107 further comprising any combination of features set forth in claims 1-104. 109. An apparatus comprising: a socket configured for detachable electrical connection with an electrical appliance; and a sensor circuit configured to sense an electrical characteristic of the connected electrical appliance through the socket; and a digital signal processor (DSP) configured to compute a plurality of data values based on the sensed electrical characteristic, the computed data values for use in controlling a security function of the socket. 110. The apparatus of 109 wherein the DSP comprises a field programmable gate array (FPGA). 111. The apparatus of any of claims 109-110 wherein the sensor circuit comprises a current and admittance sensor circuit. 112. The apparatus of any of claims 109-111 further comprising any combination of features set forth in claims 1-108. 113. A method comprising: providing power to a connected electrical appliance through a socket; sensing, via a sensor circuit, an electrical characteristic of the connected electrical appliance through the socket; computing, via a digital signal processor (DSP), a plurality of data values based on the sensed electrical characteristic; and controlling a security function of the socket based on the computed data values. 114. The method of claim 113 wherein the DSP comprises a field programmable gate array (FPGA). 115. The method of any of claims 113-114 wherein the sensed electrical characteristic comprises an admittance and a current draw of the connected electrical appliance. 116. The method of any of claims 113-115 further comprising any combination of features set forth in claims 1-108. 117. An apparatus comprising: a wireless transceiver; a socket configured for detachable electrical connection with an electrical appliance; and a circuit configured to (1) provide power to a connected electrical appliance through the socket, (2) measure an electrical characteristic of the connected electrical appliance through the socket, and (3) communicate data representative of the measured electrical characteristic to a remote computer system via the wireless transceiver. 118. The apparatus of claim 117 further comprising any combination of features set forth in claims 1-116. 119. A system comprising: a socket; a circuit; a wireless transceiver; and a computer system remote from the socket, circuit, and wireless transceiver; the socket configured for detachable electrical connection with an electrical appliance; and the circuit configured to (1) provide power to a connected electrical appliance through the socket, (2) measure an electrical characteristic of the connected electrical appliance through the socket, and (3) communicate data representative of the measured electrical characteristic to the computer system via the wireless transceiver; the computer system configured to determine whether a security condition exists for the socket based on the communicated data. 120. The system of claim 119 wherein the computer system is further configured to calibrate a security threshold for the socket based on the communicated data. 121. The system of any of claims 119-120 further comprising any combination of features set forth in claims 1-116. 122. A method comprising: providing power to a connected electrical appliance through a socket; measuring an electrical characteristic of the connected electrical appliance through the socket; and wirelessly communicating data representative of the measured electrical characteristic to a remote computer system via a wireless transceiver. 123. The method of claim 122 further comprising: the remote computer system determining whether a security condition exists for the socket based on the communicated data. 124. The method of claim 123 further comprising: the remote computer system calibrating a security threshold for the socket based on the communicated data. 125. The method of any of claims 122-124 further comprising any combination of features set forth in claims 1-116. 126. An apparatus comprising: a socket configured for detachable electrical connection with an electrical appliance; a circuit configured to (1) provide power to a connected electrical appliance through the socket, and (2) generate an alarm signal in response to a disconnection of the electrical appliance from the socket; and an interface; 

 wherein the circuit includes an isolation circuit that is configured to provide isolated DC power to the interface. 127. The apparatus of claim 126 further comprising any combination of features set forth in claims 1-125. 128. An expansion apparatus for a power strip, the apparatus comprising: a housing containing a circuit; at least one input port located on the housing and connectable to the circuit; at least one connector located on the housing and connectable to the circuit; and at least one contact located on a side wall of the housing, the at least one contact connectable to a power strip contact to communicate electronic signals between the circuit of the expansion apparatus and the power strip. 129. The apparatus of claim 128, wherein the at least one contact comprises a first contact pad connectable to a second contact pad located on the power strip. 130. The apparatus of claim 129, wherein the first and second contact pads include a plurality of spring contacts. 131. The apparatus of any of claims 128-130, wherein a plurality of input ports are located on the housing. 132. The apparatus of claim 131, wherein an internal chamber of each input port of the plurality of input ports contain at least one raised notch for attaching an external appliance to the input port. 133. The apparatus of claim 132, wherein a connector of the external appliance contains a longitudinal channel and a latitudinal channel to connect the external appliance to the input port, the connecter insertable into the input port by the longitudinal channel and lockable by rotating the connector along the latitudinal channel to secure the external appliance to the input port. 134. The apparatus any of claims 128-133, wherein a input port connects to an alarming buzzer. 135. The apparatus any of claims 128-134, wherein a input port connects to an RFID reader. 136. An expansion apparatus for a power strip, the apparatus comprising: a remote access unit configured for detachable connection with the power strip, the remote access unit configured to receive status data from the power strip and adjust status indicator for the power strip based on the received status data. 137. An expansion apparatus for a power strip, the apparatus comprising: a remote access unit configured for detachable connection with the power strip, the remote access unit configured to receive user authorization data from a user interface and provide data to the power strip in response to the received user authorization data. 138. An apparatus comprising: an expansion module for connection with a power strip as disclosed herein. 139. The apparatus of any of claims 128-138 further comprising any combination of features set forth in claims 1-127. 140. An apparatus, method, system, and/or computer program product comprising any combination of features disclosed herein.
